title: Psalms 100
subtitle: Shout for Joy to the LORD, All the Earth

sections.header: Verses 1–3 — “Know That the LORD Is God”

heb.paragraph: "Shout for joy to the LORD, all the earth. Worship the LORD with gladness; come before him with joyful songs. Know (dəʿū) that the LORD is God. It is he who made us, and we are his; we are his people, the sheep of his pasture" (vv.1–3). Five verses. The most joyful psalm in the Psalter. Three commands: shout, worship, know. The knowledge: God IS God (not debatable), he MADE us (not accidental), we are HIS (not autonomous), we are SHEEP (not self-sufficient). The theology is in the verbs: made, are, belong.

notes.note: "Shout for joy to the LORD, all the earth. Worship the LORD with gladness." The double command: volume (shout) + emotion (gladness). Worship is not solemn silence but joyful noise.

"Know that the LORD is God. It is he who made us, and we are his; we are his people, the sheep of his pasture." Four declarations: (1) The LORD is God. (2) He made us. (3) We are his. (4) We are his sheep. Identity established in four phrases.

refs.note: Being God's workmanship created for good works echoes the psalm's 'it is he who made us.'
The good shepherd knowing his sheep fulfills the psalm's 'we are the sheep of his pasture.'
Returning to the Shepherd and Overseer develops the psalm's sheep-of-his-pasture identity.

hist.context: Psalm 100 is the culmination of the enthronement psalms, calling 'all the earth' to joyful worship. The superscription 'for giving thanks' indicates liturgical function. The brief psalm (5 verses) summarizes Psalter theology: YHWH is God, Creator, Shepherd; we are his people, his sheep. It has remained central to Jewish and Christian worship.

sections.header: Verses 4–5 — “The LORD Is Good; His Love Endures Forever”

heb.paragraph: "Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with praise; give thanks to him and praise his name. For the LORD is good (ṭōb) and his love (ḥesed) endures forever; his faithfulness continues through all generations" (vv.4–5). The psalm’s closing theology in three clauses: (1) God is good. (2) His love endures forever. (3) His faithfulness spans all generations. Good, loving, faithful — and all of it permanent. Five verses, complete theology, universal joy.

notes.note: "Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with praise." The temple approach: gates first, then courts. Each step deeper is accompanied by more praise.

"For the LORD is good and his love endures forever; his faithfulness continues through all generations." Three permanences: goodness, love, faithfulness. None expires. The most concise theological summary in the Psalter.

refs.note: The refrain 'his love endures forever' develops the psalm's 'his love endures forever.'
The LORD's great love not failing and faithfulness being new every morning echoes the psalm.
Continually offering praise through lips confessing his name fulfills the psalm's thanksgiving call.

hist.context: The climactic declaration — 'the LORD is good and his love endures forever; his faithfulness continues through all generations' (v.5) — summarizes Israel's faith. This formula appears throughout Scripture as a basic confession. The psalm's global scope ('all the earth'), simple theology, and call to joyful gratitude made it universally beloved.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: " Four declarations: (1) The LORD is God…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVKnow that the Lord is God. It is he who made us, and we are his; we are his people, the sheep of his pasture.ESVKnow that the LORD, he is God! It is he who made us, and we are his; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.NIVShout for joy to the Lord, all the earth.ESVMake a joyful noise to the LORD, all the earth!

rec.text: ""Shout for joy to the LORD, all the earth. Worship the LORD with gladness." The double command: volume (shout) + emotion (gladness). Worship is not solemn silence but joyful noise."

rows.label: Good, Loving, Faithful — 100:4–5
rows.text: "Enter with thanksgiving." "The LORD is GOOD. His LOVE endures forever. His FAITHFULNESS through all generations."

The knowledge: God IS God (not debatable), he MADE us (not accidental), we are HIS (not autonomous), we are SHEEP (not self-sufficient). The theology is in the verbs: made, are, belong.

"Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with praise; give thanks to him and praise his name. themes.note: Worship peaks at 10: the most joyful psalm — five verses of pure celebration. Mercy: good, loving, faithful — all permanent. Covenant: "we are his people, the sheep of his pasture."

prayer_prompt: Shout for joy and enter His gates with thanksgiving. Consider asking God about the gratitude that should characterize your approach to Him — what thanksgiving you've neglected.
